name: Driven Brands
trigger: AI Creates More Ad Tech Frenemies; Is Social Video Eating CTV’s Lunch?
why: Brand consolidation play across 30+ sub-brands. New CEO inherited a messy multi-brand identity problem. Take 5 growing 16% YoY needs brand investment.
contact: CMO or VP Brand. New CEO brings new team.
outreach: Saw the leadership transition at Driven. We know what it looks like when a multi-brand portfolio needs to get its identity house in order — we've done it at Valvoline's scale.

recent_intel.description: Samsung Electronics America CMO Allison Stransky on ecosystem storytelling and data-driven marketing.

name: AutoZone
why: Memphis is a city where Birmingham plays well. Aggressively expanding private label and competing harder on brand against O'Reilly.
contact: VP Marketing or CMO. Research current team.
outreach: Private label brand differentiation is a different game than retail marketing. Valvoline taught us how the distributor relationship actually works from the inside.

name: Genuine Parts / NAPA
why: Atlanta is harder but not impossible with the right intro. NAPA's identity has been stagnant for years relative to O'Reilly.
contact: NAPA brand team specifically, not parent GPC.
outreach: The NAPA brand has enormous equity that isn't being fully activated. That gap is visible from outside.

name: Dorman Products
why: Dorman has been aggressively expanding beyond traditional hard parts into new categories — that's a brand architecture challenge.
outreach: When a parts brand starts expanding into new categories, the brand architecture either supports it or fights it. We've seen this problem before.

name: PGI
trigger: 15 stocks including Infosys, Tech Mahindra, HCLTech, Reliance to remain in focus
why: Brand new Memphis HQ, ~$500M revenue, wants to be seen as part of the Memphis business community. Perfect timing.
outreach: You just made Memphis home. So did we — Birmingham to Memphis is a short conversation. We know how to build brand credibility in regional markets.

recent_intel.title: 15 stocks including Infosys, Tech Mahindra, HCLTech, Reliance to remain in focus on May 4
recent_intel.description: India Inc. witnessed brisk dealmaking and expansion, with HCLTech’s AI push, Tech Mahindra’s overseas acquisition, Reliance Retail’s brand buyout, and multiple firms securing orders, appointments, and investments across telecom, energy, pharma, and hospitality

name: Advance Auto Parts
why: Advance is in a turnaround. New leadership, store closures, strategic reset. Turnarounds always surface brand questions.
outreach: Restructuring moments are brand moments. Either the identity gets sharper or it gets louder — they need the former.

name: Goodyear Tire & Rubber
why: Legacy brand actively modernizing for EV market. Cooper acquisition still being integrated. Heritage brand under pressure narrative.
outreach: The Cooper integration and EV pivot is a brand architecture challenge masquerading as a product challenge.

name: Niterra (NGK)
trigger: Rebrand from NGK to Niterra, new category expansion
why: They just rebranded the entire company from NGK to Niterra and are pivoting from spark plugs toward broader energy and mobility tech.
outreach: Rebranding a 90-year-old company name is one of the hardest things in brand strategy. The new name is the easy part.

name: Turtle Wax
why: PE-owned, launched major new product lines, trying to modernize a legacy brand.
outreach: Heritage brands with new owners always face the same tension: modernize without losing the equity that made them worth buying.

name: Holley Performance
why: Holley went public via SPAC, acquired 20+ performance brands, now trying to make sense of a messy multi-brand portfolio.
outreach: Building a performance brand house out of 20 acquisitions requires more than a holding company structure.

name: Caliber Collision
trigger: Rapid national expansion, brand scaling challenge
why: Fastest-growing collision brand in the US, PE-backed, expanding aggressively. Brand hasn't kept pace with growth.
outreach: Growing from 100 to 1,700 locations is a brand consistency problem at scale.

name: Safelite AutoGlass
why: Moving beyond glass into ADAS services — that's a brand repositioning challenge.
outreach: Adding ADAS services to a glass brand requires more than a new service menu. It requires permission.

name: Bridgestone Americas
trigger: Navigate The Next Era Of Search At Forrester’s CX Forums
why: Nashville HQ is Tier 1. Bridgestone Americas runs Firestone retail and is pushing sustainability positioning.
contact: VP Brand or Marketing, Americas division.
outreach: Nashville is home. So is understanding how a legacy tire brand earns relevance in a world increasingly skeptical of big automotive.

recent_intel.title: Navigate The Next Era Of Search At Forrester’s CX Forums
recent_intel.description: As answer engines divert discovery and squeeze the funnel, B2C marketing leaders are looking for the fastest, most durable way for their brand to become more visible and authoritative. Join us at an upcoming CX Forum to explore the new rules of visibility.

name: Prestone / Energizer Auto
why: Prestone is a legacy brand trying to modernize across a complex product portfolio.
outreach: Prestone has 95 years of equity in the garage. The question is whether that equity is being leveraged or just inherited.

name: Clarios
trigger: EV battery push, multi-brand portfolio challenge
why: Clarios owns Optima, DieHard, Varta. Complex multi-brand battery portfolio under pressure from EV transition.
outreach: Battery brands face an identity crisis — the EV transition makes their legacy positioning feel like a liability. It doesn't have to.

name: WIX Filters
trigger: NASCAR partnership activation, brand building push
why: WIX just partnered with NASCAR Racing Experience for 2025 — actively investing in brand building. Filtration category parallels Valvoline directly.
outreach: WIX just signed with NASCAR Racing Experience. We know how to make that kind of investment earn more than impressions — we've done it in the same category.

name: Highline Warren
why: PE-backed distributor on aggressive acquisition spree — bought 15+ companies and the brand is a mess. Right now moment.
contact: CEO or CMO if one exists yet.
outreach: When you're acquiring that fast, the brand either becomes the connective tissue or the liability.

name: Crash Champions
trigger: Launched technician training program, rapid expansion
why: Growing fast, investing in infrastructure including training programs — building brand from the inside out.
outreach: You just launched a technician training brand. That's the beginning of an employer brand and a customer brand story happening simultaneously.

name: Midas / TBC Corporation
why: Michelin-owned TBC runs Midas, NTB. Franchise consistency and brand expression is a chronic problem in this model.
contact: VP Marketing or Franchise Brand team.
outreach: Franchise brand consistency isn't a creative problem. It's a systems problem with creative implications.

name: Pep Boys
why: Legacy brand with complicated ownership history. Brand is confused between retail and service. Classic mess-to-solution scenario.
outreach: Pep Boys has one of the most recognizable brand assets in automotive and one of the most underactivated identities.

name: Snap-on Tools
trigger: ABC Fitness and Body Fit Training (BFT) Partner to Power Global Franchise Expans
why: Strong brand, strong internal team, not a natural agency buyer. Watch for leadership change.
outreach: Hold.

name: O'Reilly Auto Parts
why: Strong internal brand team and disciplined culture. Not a natural agency buyer. Watch for leadership change.
outreach: Hold.

name: Monro Muffler Brake
why: Brand fragmentation from acquisitions is a real problem here. Keep on radar.
outreach: Hold.

name: Stoneridge
trigger: B2B focus, limited brand need currently
why: Heavy B2B orientation, engineering culture. Not a natural agency buyer right now. Watch for leadership change.
outreach: Hold.

name: WD-40 Company
why: Tier 3 geography, stable situation. Not the right moment. Keep on radar for leadership changes.
outreach: Hold.
